event: The Queen of Sheba visits King Solomon
symbols: throne of Solomon — royal authority and divinely granted wisdom
crown of the Queen of Sheba — the dignity and wealth of a foreign sovereign
elaborate golden robes — the splendor of Solomon's court that amazed the visiting queen
columned palace — the grandeur of Solomon's kingdom built by divine blessing
gesture of presentation — the Queen bringing gifts and questions to test Solomon's wisdom
court attendants — the retinue that accompanied the Queen, itself a sign of her wealth
description: A wide panoramic painting showing a royal court scene with elaborately dressed female figures. On the left, a crowned figure sits enthroned in rich pink and gold robes. Before the throne, another crowned woman in magnificent golden and red attire extends her hand in a gesture of greeting or presentation. Several other richly dressed women with elaborate hairstyles and golden garments stand and sit around the scene. The setting includes classical columns and architectural elements suggesting a palatial interior. The palette is dominated by warm golds, reds, and pinks.
theological_description: The visit of the Queen of Sheba to Solomon, recorded in 1 Kings 10:1-13, represents one of the pinnacles of Israel's monarchy — the moment when Solomon's God-given wisdom drew the nations to Jerusalem. The Queen came 'to test him with hard questions' and was overwhelmed: 'there was no more breath in her' (1 Kings 10:5). She declared, 'The report was true that I heard in my own land of your words and of your wisdom, but I did not believe the reports until I came... Blessed be the Lord your God, who has delighted in you and set you on the throne of Israel!' (1 Kings 10:6-9). This encounter carries profound typological significance. Jesus himself invoked it in Matthew 12:42: 'The queen of the South will rise up at the judgment with this generation and condemn it, for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and behold, something greater than Solomon is here.' The Queen's journey from a distant land to seek wisdom prefigures the Gentile nations streaming to the light of the Messiah (Isaiah 60:3). Solomon in all his glory foreshadows Christ, the true Wisdom of God (1 Corinthians 1:24), to whom all nations will ultimately bow.

scripture_refs.reason: The direct biblical account of the Queen of Sheba visiting Solomon to test his wisdom
Jesus declares that something greater than Solomon is here, using the Queen of Sheba's visit as a typological precedent
God's gift of surpassing wisdom to Solomon, which drew the Queen of Sheba
Nations shall come to your light and kings to the brightness of your rising — the Queen prefiguring Gentile pilgrimage
Christ the wisdom of God — Solomon's wisdom as a type of the greater wisdom incarnate in Jesus
